name: Everett Co-Operative Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Massachusetts with branches in Everett, Lynnfield, and Woburn, offering checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, and home loans with FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Everett Co-Operative Bank is a community-focused financial institution operating in the Massachusetts area with physical locations in Everett, Lynnfield, and Woburn. The bank is a member of the FDIC and Member DIF, ensuring customer deposits are insured in full. The company is owned by parent company ECB Bancorp, Inc., which maintains investor relations for those interested in the organization's financial performance.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services, including checking and savings accounts, Certificate of Deposit (CD) products, credit cards, and home loan financing. Their current product offerings include a 6 or 24-month CD with 4.00% APY (subject to change), a 0% introductory APR credit card offering, and a promotional $20 monthly incentive for new checking account customers in Massachusetts. Personal and business customers can enroll in online banking and access mobile app functionality.

Everett Bank differentiates itself through emphasis on convenience and modern banking accessibility. The institution promotes 24/7 mobile app and online banking capabilities, allowing customers to pay bills, transfer funds, check balances, and deposit checks remotely. The bank also prioritizes customer security, with published policies explicitly stating they will never request sensitive account information via unsolicited communications and providing a direct fraud reporting number (617-387-1110).

The website content confirms Everett Bank operates as a traditional community bank with standard product offerings comparable to regional institutions. No unique lending programs, specialized credit-building tools, or distinctive financial products beyond standard banking services are advertised. The bank serves a defined geographic region and maintains traditional branch-based operations supplemented by digital banking options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with online enrollment
Business checking accounts with online enrollment
Credit cards with 0% introductory APR offer
Online banking platform with bill pay functionality
pros: FDIC insured deposits provide full deposit protection for customer funds
24/7 mobile app and online banking with bill pay, fund transfers, balance checking, and mobile check deposit
Competitive CD rates advertised at 4.00% APY for 6 or 24-month terms
0% introductory APR credit card offer available to qualified applicants
Local community bank with three physical branch locations in Massachusetts
$20 monthly promotional incentive for new checking account customers (MA only)
Published security protocols and fraud prevention guidance with direct fraud hotline
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Massachusetts locations (Everett, Lynnfield, Woburn)
Website content does not specify minimum deposit requirements for checking/savings accounts or APYs for basic deposit products
No information provided about business loan products, business credit cards, or commercial banking services beyond account types
CD rates and promotional offers explicitly subject to change without notice, limiting rate certainty
New checking account bonus ($20/month) limited to Massachusetts residents only
best_for: Massachusetts residents in or near Everett, Lynnfield, or Woburn seeking community bank services
Customers prioritizing FDIC deposit insurance and local branch accessibility
Small business owners needing basic checking and business banking services
CD investors seeking competitive rates on fixed-term deposit products
diagnosis: Everett Co-Operative Bank is best for Massachusetts residents in the Everett, Lynnfield, or Woburn areas who value community banking relationships, FDIC protection, and modern digital banking access. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank operates only three branches in Massachusetts and serves no other regions, making it unsuitable for customers outside these specific areas or those requiring multi-state banking access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
